ScriptHelpSection.headerTitle: How to copy your video script
ScriptHelpSection.headerSubtitle: Here’s how to copy the script from a YouTube video.

sectionA.title: Getting the script from YouTube (desktop)
sectionA.description: Follow these steps and you can grab the full script in about 1–2 minutes.

step1.title: Click the "···" or "More" button below the video.
step1.body: Look under the video title, next to the Like/Share buttons, for the three dots (···) or a "More" button.
step1.imageAlt: Location of the More (···) button on the YouTube watch page.

step2.title: In the menu, choose "Show transcript" or "Transcript".
step2.body: In the menu that opens, click the item named "Show transcript", "Captions", or "Transcript".
step2.imageAlt: YouTube menu showing the Show transcript option.

step3.title: Select the entire transcript on the right and copy it.
step3.tip1: 1. Click once on the first line of the transcript.
step3.tip2Part1: 2. Scroll all the way down, then
step3.tip2Part2: click the last line once to select the entire transcript.
step3.tip3: 3. Press Ctrl + C (or ⌘ + C on Mac) to copy.
step3.tipNote: ※ Using Ctrl + A selects the whole page, so we don’t recommend it when you only want the transcript.
step3.imageAlt: Selecting the entire transcript in the YouTube transcript panel.

step4.title: Paste it into the input box on the left of this page.
step4.body: Click the "Paste video script" input area, then press Ctrl + V (or ⌘ + V on Mac) to paste. Now you’re ready.
step4.imageAlt: Pasting text into the video script input field.

sectionB.title: Want to use subtitles or an editing tool instead?
sectionB.description: If you already have a project in progress, you can simply copy the text and paste it here.

case1.title: Copying subtitles or script from an editor (Premiere, CapCut, etc.)
case1.body: Select all subtitle text from the timeline, copy it, and paste it into the input on the left.

case2.title: Opening subtitle files (srt, vtt, etc.) as text
case2.body: Open the srt/vtt file in a text editor like Notepad. It will contain timestamps with dialogue. Copy the lines you need and paste them here.

case3.title: Using a script document you’ve already written
case3.body: It doesn’t matter where your script lives—blog, Word, Notion, etc. Just copy the full text and paste it as-is.

faq.title: Q. I don’t see a "Show transcript" / "Transcript" menu.
faq.item1: Some videos may hide the transcript at the creator’s choice.
faq.item2: There may be no subtitles at all, or it might be a live/real-time stream.
faq.item3: In that case, you’ll need to type it manually while listening, or use a separate subtitle generation tool.

CreatorSection.label: Creator
CreatorSection.name: Lina Lee
CreatorSection.quote: “There’s always more to watch than there is time. I’d finish a video thinking it was incredibly helpful, but by the evening I could barely remember half of it. I kept telling myself I’d come back to it later, and of course many of those videos just disappeared into the backlog. With health, kids’ education, and personal finance all competing for attention, it became even easier to lose track. So I built a tool myself. When you save what you just watched as a “structure map”, you can reopen it later and still see the flow and the key points at a glance.”
